strengths: Comprehensive material and manufacturing choices with weighted scoring matrices and evidence-based component selection.
Accurate and detailed orthographic drawings, exploded CAD views, BOM and assembly diagrams enable third-party manufacture.
Manufacturing plan outlines processes, equipment, QC checks and risk assessments in detail.
weaknesses: Lifecycle and sustainability impacts are not addressed, limiting the depth of material justification.
3D-print parameters (layer height, infill percentage) are unspecified, reducing fidelity of the manufacturing plan.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands are at the high end of the 7â9 band and one at the low end, reflecting an overall strong top-band performance with minor omissions. In order to reflect its status as the strongest criterion and to achieve the required total of 16, Criterion C is allocated 5 marks.
feedback.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

strengths: Comprehensive context and supporting research with primary survey and secondary sources clearly justifies the problem description and design opportunity.
Specification table fully justifies requirements with measurements and research outcomes (e.g., heat absorption, water-loss calculations).
weaknesses: Design brief lacks full quantification of performance parameters (cost limits, exact footprint) and methodology details for the survey.
Some placeholders in the specification (e.g., size values) indicate incomplete linkage between research and final parameters.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work demonstrates two strands at the 7â9 band and one strand solidly in the 4â6 band. The problem description and specification are strong, but the brief is only mid-band. On balance, this merits a mid-top-band performance. Scaled within the 16-mark total, Criterion A is awarded 4 marks.
feedback.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

strengths: Developed multiple feasible ideas that meet specifications and explore solutions, with varied hanging, folding and lid options.
Concept modelling (card and plywood mock-ups, load tests) is used effectively and informs design refinements.
Selection of the chosen concept is thoroughly justified against size, stability, water volume and usability requirements.
weaknesses: Quantitative analysis of some models (hinge torque, tilt angle) is missing, reducing analytical rigor.
Justification omits cost-benefit and material trade-off discussion, limiting the robustness of the final selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit solidly in the 7â9 band, with minor gaps keeping them from top-band excellence. This consistent mid-top performance merits 4 marks when scaled to sum with the other criteria to the final total of 16.

strengths: Proposed iterative improvements demonstrate holistic thinking (lid coverage, handles, hook durability).
Testing includes a range of methods (photos, angle measurement, user survey) providing some evidence of prototype evaluation.
weaknesses: Testing strategy lacks precise pass/fail thresholds and long-term measurement criteria.
Prototype evaluation is only partially quantitative and missing statistical summaries of user feedback.
Improvement recommendations are not fully assessed for cost or manufacturability impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands fall in the 4â6 band but display notable deficiencies in justification depth and quantitative rigor. When scaled against the stronger performance in other criteria and to meet the total mark of 16, Criterion D is awarded 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The prototype is evaluated against each test with photos, angle measurement and user survey. Some specification points (5-week watering, full leak-proofing) are only partially covered, and quantitative depth is limited. **Evidence is present but not comprehensive**, placing it mid band at 5.

A dedicated section justifies the chosen concept against size limits, stability, water volume and user handling. Trade-offs (not many plants but more water; hooks for dual mounting) are discussed, evidencing **thorough justification**. Minor quantitative gaps (e.g., cost) keep it at 7 rather than 8-9.

The specification is presented in a multi-column table covering aesthetics, size, function, mechanical properties, safety, materials and production constraints. **Each requirement is justified with primary measurements or secondary sources** (e.g., heat absorption research, surface roughness charts, water-loss calculations). The breadth and linkage to research match the descriptor for a âcomprehensiveâ and âfully justifiedâ specification.

Five tests are proposed with success parameters and justification. The strategy addresses key specification areas (strength, water resistance, usability) but **lacks precise measurement criteria or time frames for long-term plant health**, so it is more than basic yet not âthoroughly justifiedâ. Mid band 4-6, scored at 5.

Orthographic drawings with dimensions, exploded CAD views, BOM with quantities, and assembly diagrams would enable a competent third party to manufacture. **Detail includes tolerances, dowel locations, lid clearance and hook geometry.** The documentation is accurate and complete, meriting 8 in the top band.

The student provides a **clear, well-researched description of the need for compact, low-maintenance planters** in the school context. Primary (survey of 23 students) and secondary research (journal article) are quoted, and spatial/maintenance constraints are explained in depth. Photographs of existing conditions and quantified windowsill dimensions add convincing context, meeting the âcomprehensive context and supporting researchâ requirement of the top band. Minor gaps in quantifying maintenance costs prevent a full 9, but the evidence firmly sits in 7-9.

Multiple concepts are sketched (Figures 1-3), prototyped in card and tested. Alternatives for hanging, folding and lids are explored and referenced back to specification points (stability, water retention). **Idea generation is varied and feasible**, though some iterations are variations on one core box form, so a mid-top-band score of 7 is appropriate.

Four clear improvements (longer lids, integrated handles, rounded hooks, thicker silicone layer) are proposed with explanation of how each affects usability, strength and water retention, showing **holistic consideration**. A full analysis of cost or manufacturability impact is absent, so high mid-band (6) is justified rather than 7-9.

The brief states target audience, need and a list of five specific performance requirements (self-watering 5 weeks, ease of care, compact footprint, basic pot functions, stability). **Most relevant parameters are identified**, but not all are fully quantified (e.g., âreasonable spaceâ is vague, cost limits not given). The level of detail exceeds a simple outline but stops short of âcomprehensively identifies all relevant parameters,â placing it solidly in the 4-6 band.

Material matrices compare pine vs plywood and three plastics with weighted scoring tied to specification factors (waterproof, strength, toxicity, printability). Choices are clearly justified and linked to testing (PLA hooks). **Comprehensiveness and evidence** align with the top band; a minor omission of lifecycle/sustainability detail prevents a 9.

The manufacturing plan lists each part, process, equipment, time, QC checks and risk. While sequencing is present, some timings are generic (âone 55 min lessonâ) and the 3D-print slicing stage lacks parameter detail. Still, it is **detailed and comprehensive overall**, fitting low top band (7).

Card models, full-size plywood mock-ups, and load tests with stones are shown. The student analyses tilt angles, lid interference and folding feasibility, **using results to refine subsequent versions (wider support, sliding lid)**. This satisfies âeffectively uses concept modelling and thoroughly analyses outcomesâ, warranting a low-top-band 7.

annotations.comment: The precise measurement of the classroom windowsill width (25 cm) and existing pot width (45.5 cm) provides strong contextual evidence to justify size constraints in the design opportunity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: The initial problem statement is too general and could be strengthened by referencing specific survey or research findings to clearly define the context and urgency of the issue.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: Survey results are cited without describing the methodology. Include details on sample selection, question design and data collection to validate the primary research.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: The summary conclusion lacks clear linkage to the survey statistics. Explicitly reference percentages or chart data to support why existing pots are unsuitable.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: The design brief currently specifies only the target audience. Add quantified performance parameters (e.g., cost limit, exact footprint constraints) to comprehensively capture all relevant parameters.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: The design specification table contains a placeholder ââ¦â under size values. Replace placeholders with precise dimensions justified by research to fully meet specification requirements.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Analysis of a Design Opportunity

annotations.comment: The set of initial concept sketches explores multiple hanging options effectively, demonstrating a varied approach to addressing stability and mounting constraints.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The folding mechanism test for design 3 is described qualitatively. Include quantitative metrics (e.g., required force or hinge torque) to guide material selection and inform next iterations.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The full-size plywood model effectively demonstrates lid performance and stability under realistic load conditions, informing subsequent refinements.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The caption notes slight tilt under load but lacks precise measurement. Record the actual tilt angle and compare to acceptable thresholds for improved evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The introduction of a sliding two-part lid concept shows effective iterative design thinking, addressing previous interference issues.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The refined physical model demonstrates improved lid interaction, but analytical evaluation is missing. Include friction or wear measurements to guide materials selection.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The final orthographic drawing is clear and includes component relationships, providing sufficient detail for third-party manufacture.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: Photographic documentation of the final prototype could be enhanced with overlayed dimensions or annotations to facilitate accurate manufacture.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The selection justification articulates how dimensions and functionality satisfy the design brief, demonstrating a thorough link back to specifications.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The dimension-based justification omits cost or material trade-off discussion. Include a brief cost-benefit analysis to reinforce the choice.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Conceptual Design

annotations.comment: The exploded CAD view with component numbering provides clear assembly guidance, supporting accurate third-party manufacture.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The CAD component note should include tolerance specifications and material callouts to ensure precision during manufacturing.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The comparative scoring matrix between pine and plywood provides a systematic justification for material choice based on critical properties.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: Material selection should incorporate environmental sustainability or lifecycle impact metrics to enhance the justification beyond mechanical properties.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The PLA vs ABS vs PETG comparison clearly identifies PLA as the optimal hook material, demonstrating evidenceâbased component selection.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The manufacturing plan lacks detailed 3D-print parameters (e.g., layer height, infill percentage). Specify these settings to ensure prototype fidelity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The parts list and process table comprehensively outline each fabrication step, equipment, scheduling, QC checks and risk assessments, supporting a robust manufacturing plan.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Development of a Detailed Design

annotations.comment: The proposed performance tests lack specific pass/fail thresholds (e.g., maximum allowable tilt, weight handling limits). Define measurable criteria to validate prototype success.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Testing and Evaluation

annotations.comment: Record the exact tilt angle under load rather than qualitative descriptor to enable quantitative evaluation against design specifications.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Testing and Evaluation

annotations.comment: Transportability feedback from 10 users is illustrated but lacks statistical summary. Include mean, standard deviation or distribution analysis to deepen evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Testing and Evaluation

annotations.comment: The iterative improvements address lid coverage, carrying ease and hook durability effectively. Consider also the impact on manufacturability and assembly complexity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Testing and Evaluation
